output.text:
Control Coolify from your terminal or integrate with AI assistants
like Claude to debug deployments and manage infrastructure with
natural language.

Real-time Terminal

You do not need to leave your browser to manage your servers.
Coolify's real-time terminal allows you to run commands on your
servers directly from your browser.

Collaborative

Share your projects with your team members and work together on
them. You can also control permissions and roles for each member.

Pull Request Deployments

Automagically deploy new commits and pull requests separately to
quickly review contributions and speed up your teamwork!

Server Automations

Once your server is connected, Coolify will handle numerous tasks
automatically, allowing you to concentrate on your code.

Monitoring

Coolify will keep an eye on your deployments, servers, disk usage,
and other aspects, and notify you if any issues arise.

Notifications

Coolify will notify you through your chosen channels like Discord,
Telegram, and email in case of any events on your server or with
your deployments.

[Jump to FeaturesJump to Features](https://coolify.io/#features)

Any Server

You can deploy your resources to any server, including your own
servers, VPS, Raspberry Pi, EC2, DigitalOcean, Linode, Hetzner, and
more. All you need is an SSH connection.

Any Use-Case

You can deploy your resources (apps, databases, services) to a
single server or multiple servers according to your requirements.

Any Service

You can deploy any service you want that is compatible with Docker.
Also there are lots of one-click services available to deploy.

See the full list [here](https://coolify.io/docs/services/overview?utm_source=coolify.io).

Push to deploy

Nowadays, Git integration is considered the norm. We provide
integration with both hosted and self-hosted platforms like GitHub,
GitLab, Bitbucket, Gitea, and others.

Free SSL Certificates

Coolify takes care of automatically setting up and renewing Let's
Encrypt SSL certificates for your custom domains, so you can relax
and not have to deal with it.

No vendor lock-in

You have complete control over your data. All settings are saved on
your own servers, giving you the ability to oversee everything even
if you decide to stop using Coolify (oh nooo).

Automatic DB Backups

Your data is automatically backed up to any S3 compatible solution,
allowing you to easily restore it with just a few clicks if any
problems occur.

Webhooks

Integrate into your CI/CD pipelines or create custom integrations
using Github Actions, Gitlab CI, Bitbucket Pipelines, or any other
CI/CD tool of your choice.

Powerful API

Coolify offers a robust API that enables you to automate
deployments, manage resources, and integrate with your current
tools, providing limitless possibilities.

[Command Line Tool](https://github.com/coollabsio/coolify-cli)

Control Coolify from your terminal or integrate with AI assistants
like Claude to debug deployments and manage infrastructure with
natural language.

Real-time Terminal

# Welcome to Dokploy

Copy as Markdown

Dokploy is a open source alternative to Heroku, Vercel, and Netlify.

Dokploy is a stable, easy-to-use deployment solution designed to simplify the application management process. Think of Dokploy as your free self hostable alternative to platforms like Heroku, Vercel, and Netlify, leveraging the robustness of [Docker](https://www.docker.com/) and the flexibility of [Traefik](https://traefik.io/).

![Logo](https://docs.dokploy.com/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Flogo.1b49551d.png&w=3840&q=75)

## [Why Choose Dokploy?](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core\#why-choose-dokploy)

- **Simplicity:** Easy setup and management of deployments.
- **Flexibility:** Supports a wide range of applications and databases.
- **Open Source:** Free and open-source software, available for anyone to use.

## [Setting up Dokploy](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core\#setting-up-dokploy)

Getting started with Dokploy is straightforward. Follow our guides to install and configure your applications and databases effectively.

## [Setting up](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core\#setting-up)

Please go to get started.

[**Installation** \\
Learn how to install Dokploy.](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core/installation) [**Applications** \\
Learn how to deploy applications.](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core/applications) [**Databases** \\
Learn how to deploy databases.](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core/databases) [**Docker Compose** \\
Learn how to use Docker Compose with Dokploy.](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core/docker-compose)

[Architecture of Dokploy\\
\\
Overview of the core architecture components of Dokploy.](https://docs.dokploy.com/docs/core/architecture)

Control Coolify from your terminal or integrate with AI assistants
like Claude to debug deployments and manage infrastructure with
natural language.

Real-time Terminal

You do not need to leave your browser to manage your servers.
Coolify's real-time terminal allows you to run commands on your
servers directly from your browser.

Collaborative

Share your projects with your team members and work together on
them. You can also control permissions and roles for each member.

Pull Request Deployments

Automagically deploy new commits and pull requests separately to
quickly review contributions and speed up your teamwork!

Server Automations

Once your server is connected, Coolify will handle numerous tasks
automatically, allowing you to concentrate on your code.

Monitoring

Coolify will keep an eye on your deployments, servers, disk usage,
and other aspects, and notify you if any issues arise.

Notifications

Coolify will notify you through your chosen channels like Discord,
Telegram, and email in case of any events on your server or with
your deployments.

[Jump to FeaturesJump to Features](https://coolify.io/#features)

Any Server

You can deploy your resources to any server, including your own
servers, VPS, Raspberry Pi, EC2, DigitalOcean, Linode, Hetzner, and
more. All you need is an SSH connection.

Any Use-Case

You can deploy your resources (apps, databases, services) to a
single server or multiple servers according to your requirements.

Any Service

You can deploy any service you want that is compatible with Docker.
Also there are lots of one-click services available to deploy.

See the full list [here](https://coolify.io/docs/services/overview?utm_source=coolify.io).

Push to deploy

Nowadays, Git integration is considered the norm. We provide
integration with both hosted and self-hosted platforms like GitHub,
GitLab, Bitbucket, Gitea, and others.

Free SSL Certificates

Coolify takes care of automatically setting up and renewing Let's
Encrypt SSL certificates for your custom domains, so you can relax
and not have to deal with it.

No vendor lock-in

You have complete control over your data. All settings are saved on
your own servers, giving you the ability to oversee everything even
if you decide to stop using Coolify (oh nooo).

Automatic DB Backups

Your data is automatically backed up to any S3 compatible solution,
allowing you to easily restore it with just a few clicks if any
problems occur.

Webhooks

Integrate into your CI/CD pipelines or create custom integrations
using Github Actions, Gitlab CI, Bitbucket Pipelines, or any other
CI/CD tool of your choice.

Powerful API

Coolify offers a robust API that enables you to automate
deployments, manage resources, and integrate with your current
tools, providing limitless possibilities.

[Command Line Tool](https://github.com/coollabsio/coolify-cli)

like Claude to debug deployments and manage infrastructure with
natural language.

# Welcome to Dokploy

Dokploy is a open source alternative to Heroku, Vercel, and Netlify.

Dokploy is a stable, easy-to-use deployment solution designed to simplify the application management process. Think of Dokploy as your free self hostable alternative to platforms like Heroku, Vercel, and Netlify, leveraging the robustness of [Docker](https://www.docker.com/) and the flexibility of [Traefik](https://traefik.io/).

![Logo](https://docs.dokploy.com/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Flogo.1b49551d.png&w=3840&q=75)

- **Simplicity:** Easy setup and management of deployments.
- **Flexibility:** Supports a wide range of applications and databases.
- **Open Source:** Free and open-source software, available for anyone to use.

Getting started with Dokploy is straightforward. Follow our guides to install and configure your applications and databases effectively.

event.text:
Here’s a quick, practical comparison based on the information in the sources you provided:

What each tool is best for
- Coolify (Self-hosted, open-source, with cloud options)
  - Strengths:
    - Full-stack self-hosted deployment platform with a wide range of services and one-click apps.
    - Git integration with hosted/self-hosted Git providers.
    - Real-time terminal for in-browser server management.
    - Collaboration features (permissions, roles) for teams.
    - Pull request deployments and automated server deployments.
    - Monitoring and notifications (disk usage, deployments, alerts via Discord/Telegram/email).
    - Automated DB backups to S3-compatible storage.
    - Powerful API for automations and integrations.
    - Works with many servers (SSH-based) and supports many services compatible with Docker.
  - Ideal if you want an all-in-one, feature-rich self-hosted platform with strong collaboration, deployment automation, and observability.

- Dokploy (Open-source deployment platform, self-hosted)
  - Strengths:
    - Open-source, self-hostable alternative to platforms like Heroku, Vercel, Netlify.
    - Emphasizes simplicity and flexibility for deploying apps and databases.
    - Built around Docker and Traefik, aiming for straightforward setup and management.
    - Supports applications, databases, and Docker Compose workflows.
  - Ideal if you want a lean, open-source solution focused on easy setup and Docker-based deployments, with emphasis on being self-hosted and flexible.

Which one should you use?
- If you need a feature-rich, collaborative, multi-service deployment platform with strong automation, monitoring, and extensive integrations (PR deployments, webhooks, notifications, in-browser terminal, etc.), choose Coolify.
- If you want a simpler, open-source, self-hosted deployment tool with a focus on easy setup and Docker/Traefik-based deployments for apps and databases, choose Dokploy.

Practical quick-start guidance
- Pick Coolify if:
  - You’re managing multiple services or projects and value collaboration, real-time server management, and built-in monitoring.
  - You want robust deployment workflows and integrations (Git, CI/CD hooks, PR-based deployments).
- Pick Dokploy if:
  - You prefer a leaner, straightforward self-hosted solution and are comfortable with Docker/Traefik-based deployment.
  - You mainly need to deploy apps and databases with Docker Compose support in a self-hosted environment.

Notes
- Both sources are marked as completed, so the above descriptions are based on their stated features and positioning.

If you share more about your exact use case (number of apps, team size, need for collaboration vs. simplicity, target environment, etc.), I can give a more tailored recommendation.

content.text:
**Comparing deployment platforms**

I have content from both Coolify, a self-hosted deployment platform, and Dokploy, which is an open-source alternative to Heroku/Vercel. Choosing between them really depends on specific needs like Docker support, simplicity, and database requirements.

Coolify offers features like Git integration, SSL, monitoring, and real-time terminal capabilities, while Dokploy is simpler and focuses on open-source options. If more features are needed, Coolify is the way to go; for a straightforward approach, Dokploy is a solid choice.

Here’s a quick, practical comparison based on the information in the sources you provided:

What each tool is best for
- Coolify (Self-hosted, open-source, with cloud options)
  - Strengths:
    - Full-stack self-hosted deployment platform with a wide range of services and one-click apps.
    - Git integration with hosted/self-hosted Git providers.
    - Real-time terminal for in-browser server management.
    - Collaboration features (permissions, roles) for teams.
    - Pull request deployments and automated server deployments.
    - Monitoring and notifications (disk usage, deployments, alerts via Discord/Telegram/email).
    - Automated DB backups to S3-compatible storage.
    - Powerful API for automations and integrations.
    - Works with many servers (SSH-based) and supports many services compatible with Docker.
  - Ideal if you want an all-in-one, feature-rich self-hosted platform with strong collaboration, deployment automation, and observability.

- Dokploy (Open-source deployment platform, self-hosted)
  - Strengths:
    - Open-source, self-hostable alternative to platforms like Heroku, Vercel, Netlify.
    - Emphasizes simplicity and flexibility for deploying apps and databases.
    - Built around Docker and Traefik, aiming for straightforward setup and management.
    - Supports applications, databases, and Docker Compose workflows.
  - Ideal if you want a lean, open-source solution focused on easy setup and Docker-based deployments, with emphasis on being self-hosted and flexible.

Which one should you use?
- If you need a feature-rich, collaborative, multi-service deployment platform with strong automation, monitoring, and extensive integrations (PR deployments, webhooks, notifications, in-browser terminal, etc.), choose Coolify.
- If you want a simpler, open-source, self-hosted deployment tool with a focus on easy setup and Docker/Traefik-based deployments for apps and databases, choose Dokploy.

Practical quick-start guidance
- Pick Coolify if:
  - You’re managing multiple services or projects and value collaboration, real-time server management, and built-in monitoring.
  - You want robust deployment workflows and integrations (Git, CI/CD hooks, PR-based deployments).
- Pick Dokploy if:
  - You prefer a leaner, straightforward self-hosted solution and are comfortable with Docker/Traefik-based deployment.
  - You mainly need to deploy apps and databases with Docker Compose support in a self-hosted environment.

Notes
- Both sources are marked as completed, so the above descriptions are based on their stated features and positioning.

If you share more about your exact use case (number of apps, team size, need for collaboration vs. simplicity, target environment, etc.), I can give a more tailored recommendation.
